Gets the List of Apps for AB-Extend Customer

delete.description:
Delete App by given DeploymentID

This endpoint intended to delete ECR repo, ECR manifests, service images, uninstall helm-release, 
and update the deleted_at in DB by given App Name.

Required: Valid Access Token

get.description: Gets the App By Name

put.summary: Creates new App for AB-Extend Customers

post.description:
Creates Deployment for extend service

This endpoint intended to apply the helm release or deploying the custom service image.
Logic in this endpoint:
	- Getting the repo from AppHistory table and image tag from request
	- Getting the Helm Manifest template from S3
	- Filling the Helm Manifest values
	- Installing the Helm Manifest to deploy the custom service image into K8s based on given namespace
	- Storing the filled Helm Manifest to S3
	- Storing the Deployment information to Database

Required: Valid Access Token

delete.description: Deletes the images uploaded to the repository

Get a list of container images

Default 'cached' parameter is 'true'

get.description:
Get list of environment secrets per app
Available Deployment Status: 
<code>deployed</code> = app config is already deployed
<code>undeployed</code> = app config is not deployed yet and need restart and deploy of the app to be deployed

post.description:
Save an environment secret.
      Request body:
   - configName		: environment secret name - Required.
   - source			: source of the configuration value (plaintext or ssm) - Required.
   - value			: configuration value - Required.
   - description	: description of the configuration - Optional.
   - applyMask		: mask the value in the Helm manifest for sensitive information (true or false) - Optional.

delete.description: Delete an environment secret.

put.description:
Update an environment secret.
      Request body:
   - value			: configuration value - Required.
   - description	: description of the configuration - Optional.
   - applyMask		: mask the value in the Helm manifest for sensitive information (true or false) - Optional.

parameters.name: body

delete.description:
Delete extend app by given {app} name

This endpoint will delete app information, configuration, deployments and all related manifest from 
db, storage and cluster

parameters.description: Force app deletion, if 'true' will proceed delete regardless of current app status (default: false)
parameters.name: forced

patch.description: Update app info provided on request body%!(EXTRA string=ADMIN:NAMESPACE:{namespace}:EXTEND:APP, string=UPDATE)

post.description:
Idempotent endpoint that creates or updates an Extend app from a declarative spec.
Uses three-way merge semantics (kubectl apply) for variables, secrets, and permissions.

Note: <code>preferred_k8s_namespace</code> is only used on the initial create call; it is ignored on subsequent apply calls.
On the first apply after creation the deletion phase is skipped (no prior apply state recorded).
post.summary: Declaratively create or update an extend app from a spec

delete.description: Unsubscribe App queue from a topic by Topic Name.

post.description:
Generates a new credential for an extend app that is already integrated with a KeyValue cluster.
Replaces the old credential. Integration status will transition to MODIFYING, then READY when complete.
If the same username and password are provided, returns the existing credential (idempotent).

Acknowledgement for secure credential handling is only required at integrate-app; not needed for this endpoint.
post.summary: Generate a new credential for an Extend App integrated with a KeyValue cluster

delete.description: Remove integration of an extend app with a cluster. Removes key-value env/secret from app, deletes credential and AWS user.
delete.summary: Remove Integration of an Extend App from a Cluster

get.description: Get integration of an extend app with a cluster.

post.description: Integrate an Extend App with an existing KeyValue cluster. Injects REDIS_HOST, REDIS_PORT, REDIS_USERNAME, REDIS_PASSWORD to the app config.
post.summary: Integrate Extend App with KeyValue Cluster

post.description:
Creates a new database credential for the customer. This will soft-delete the old credential and create a new one.

<code>acknowledgements.acceptNosqlSecureCredentialHandling</code> is optional when previously accepted during database creation. Otherwise, it MUST be set to true to proceed with credential creation, indicating the customer accepts the secure credential handling mechanism.
post.summary: Creates a new database credential for the customer

post.description:
Creates a new database credential for the customer. This will soft-delete the old credential and create a new one.

<code>acknowledgements.acceptNosqlSecureCredentialHandling</code> is optional when previously accepted during database creation. Otherwise, it MUST be set to true to proceed with credential creation, indicating the customer accepts the secure credential handling mechanism.
post.summary: Creates a new database credential for the customer

delete.description: Deletes a NoSQL database and its credentials associated with given extend app and game namespace. The database will be removed from the provisioned NoSQL cluster.
delete.summary: Deletes NoSQL Database for Extend App

get.description:
Get a NoSQL database information returns the NoSQL database related information by given game namespace
and app name.

<code>resourceStatus</code> field - indicates the NoSQL cluster status:
- <code>updating</code> : The cluster is being modified and is not yet accessible (e.g., updating min/max DCU). 
- <code>creating</code> : The cluster or instance is being created and is not yet accessible. 
- <code>deleting</code> : The cluster is in the process of being deleted and is not accessible. 
- <code>failed</code> : The cluster failed to provision or is in an error state and not accessible. 
- <code>stopping</code> : The cluster is in the process of stopping and will soon become inaccessible. 
- <code>stopped</code> : The cluster is stopped and not accessible. 
- <code>starting</code> : The cluster is transitioning from stopped to running, or is rebooting. 
- <code>unknown</code> : The cluster status is not recognized 
- <code>available</code> : The cluster is accessible. 
- <code>maintenance</code> : The cluster is undergoing maintenance operations and is not accessible.
get.summary: Get NoSQL Database for Extend App

post.description:
Creates a NoSQL database along with its credentials associated with given extend app. The database will be created in the provisioned NoSQL cluster.

<code>acknowledgements.acceptNosqlSecureCredentialHandling</code> MUST be set to true to proceed with database creation, indicating the customer accepts the secure credential handling mechanism.
post.summary: Creates NoSQL Database for Extend App

post.description: Update app resources provided on request body

post.description: Creates a new database credential for the customer. This will soft-delete the old credential and create a new one.
post.summary: Creates a new database credential for the customer

delete.description: Deletes a SQL database and its credentials associated with given extend app and game namespace. The database will be removed from the provisioned SQL cluster.
delete.summary: Deletes SQL Database for Extend App

get.description:
Get a SQL database information returns the SQL database related information by given game namespace
and app name.

<code>resourceStatus</code> field - indicates the SQL cluster status:
- <code>updating</code> : The cluster is being modified and is not yet accessible (e.g., updating min/max ACU). 
- <code>failed</code> : The cluster failed to provision or is in an error state and not accessible. 
- <code>stopping</code> : The cluster is in the process of stopping and will soon become inaccessible. 
- <code>stopped</code> : The cluster is stopped and not accessible. 
- <code>starting</code> : The cluster is transitioning from stopped to running, or is rebooting. 
- <code>creating</code> : The cluster or instance is being created and is not yet accessible. 
- <code>deleting</code> : The cluster is in the process of being deleted and is not accessible. 
- <code>maintenance</code> : The cluster is undergoing maintenance operations and is not accessible. 
- <code>unknown</code> : The cluster status is not recognized 
- <code>configuring-replica</code> : The cluster is configuring replica instances for multi-AZ deployment. 
- <code>available</code> : The cluster is accessible.
get.summary: Get SQL Database for Extend App

post.description: Creates a SQL database along with its credentials associated with given extend app. The database will be created in the provisioned SQL cluster.
post.summary: Creates SQL Database for Extend App

This endpoint intended to get Deployment information.

Get Key-Value cluster information returns the Key-Value cluster related information by given studio/publisher namespace.

<code>status</code> field - indicates the Key-Value cluster status.

List Key-Value clusters by studio/publisher namespace.
If <code>refresh=true</code>, the service will sync cluster status from the provider (cached).

post.description:
Provision Key-Value cluster using AWS Valkey Serverless that can be used by extend apps in game namespace within the studio.

Cluster limits:
- Private cloud: up to 10 clusters per studio namespace
- Shared cloud: 1 cluster per studio namespace

Provisioning process is done asynchronously, you can check from the <code>status</code> field of the resource response.

get.description: Get Key-Value cluster limit config.

delete.description:
Delete Key-Value cluster. Only allowed when cluster status is AVAILABLE or CREATE-FAILED.
Force-deletes all active integrations. Returns resource with status DELETING.

put.description:
Update Key-Value cluster configuration (maxDataStorageGB, maxECPUPerSecond, profileName).
Only allowed when cluster status is AVAILABLE. Process is asynchronous; poll Get Cluster for updated status.

get.description: Get the list of Extend Apps integrated with a specific Key-Value cluster. Only active integrations are returned.
get.summary: Get List Extend Apps Integrated with a Key-Value Cluster

delete.description:
You can only delete the cluster when its status is "available".

Deleting the cluster will:
	- Remove NoSQL integration.
	- Remove all the data from the NoSQL
	- Remove all the credentials, including the Extend App’s credentials.

get.description:
Get NoSQL cluster information returns the NoSQL cluster related information by given studio/publisher namespace.

<code>status</code> field - indicates the NoSQL cluster status:
- <code>unknown</code> : The cluster status is not recognized 
- <code>available</code> : The cluster is accessible. 
- <code>maintenance</code> : The cluster is undergoing maintenance operations and is not accessible. 
- <code>updating</code> : The cluster is being modified and is not yet accessible (e.g., updating min/max DCU). 
- <code>creating</code> : The cluster or instance is being created and is not yet accessible. 
- <code>deleting</code> : The cluster is in the process of being deleted and is not accessible. 
- <code>failed</code> : The cluster failed to provision or is in an error state and not accessible. 
- <code>stopping</code> : The cluster is in the process of stopping and will soon become inaccessible. 
- <code>stopped</code> : The cluster is stopped and not accessible. 
- <code>starting</code> : The cluster is transitioning from stopped to running, or is rebooting.

post.description:
Provision NoSQL database cluster and instances that can be used by extend apps in game namespace within the studio.
Only one NoSQL resource can be created for one studio/publisher namespace.

Provisioning process is done asynchronously, you can check from the <code>status</code> field of the resource response.

put.description:
Update NoSQL cluster configurations such as min/max DCU (Database Capacity Units) for the NoSQL cluster in the given studio/publisher namespace.
The cluster must be in an available state to allow configuration updates.

Configuration update process may take some time to complete, you can check the updated status from the <code>status</code> field of the resource response.

Start NoSQL cluster.
You can only start the cluster when its status is "stopped".

Cluster starting process may take some time to complete.

Stop NoSQL cluster.
You can only start the cluster when its status is "available".

Cluster stopping process may take some time to complete.

get.description: Returns the NoSQL access tunnel information for a specified game namespace

This endpoint return the Extend Apps Configurable Limits for front end to use for initial validation before app creation
e.g. 
Replica Limit will be used to check max replica that can be created for the said environment by default
CPU Limit will be used to validate max allowed CPU for the extend app that hasn't been created 
Memory Limit will be used to validate max allowed Memory for the extend app that hasn't been created 
MaxAppNotificationSubscription will be used to validate the maximum number of subscriber for an app status notification
get.summary: Get Extend Apps Configurable Limits e.g. any kind of limits for front end validation use

delete.description:
You can only delete the cluster when its status is "available".

Deleting the cluster will:
	- Remove SQL integration.
	- Remove all the data from the SQL
	- Remove all the credentials, including the Extend App’s credentials.

get.description:
Get SQL cluster information returns the SQL cluster related information by given studio/publisher namespace.

<code>status</code> field - indicates the SQL cluster status:
- <code>starting</code> : The cluster is transitioning from stopped to running, or is rebooting. 
- <code>creating</code> : The cluster or instance is being created and is not yet accessible. 
- <code>deleting</code> : The cluster is in the process of being deleted and is not accessible. 
- <code>maintenance</code> : The cluster is undergoing maintenance operations and is not accessible. 
- <code>unknown</code> : The cluster status is not recognized 
- <code>configuring-replica</code> : The cluster is configuring replica instances for multi-AZ deployment. 
- <code>available</code> : The cluster is accessible. 
- <code>updating</code> : The cluster is being modified and is not yet accessible (e.g., updating min/max ACU). 
- <code>failed</code> : The cluster failed to provision or is in an error state and not accessible. 
- <code>stopping</code> : The cluster is in the process of stopping and will soon become inaccessible. 
- <code>stopped</code> : The cluster is stopped and not accessible.

post.description:
Provision SQL database cluster and instances that can be used by extend apps in game namespace within the studio.
Only one SQL resource can be created for one studio/publisher namespace.

Provisioning process is done asynchronously, you can check from the <code>status</code> field of the resource response.

put.description:
Update SQL cluster configurations such as min/max ACU (AuroraDB Capacity Units) for the SQL cluster in the given studio/publisher namespace.
The cluster must be in an available state to allow configuration updates.

Configuration update process may take some time to complete, you can check the updated status from the <code>status</code> field of the resource response.

Start SQL cluster.
You can only start the cluster when its status is "stopped".

Cluster starting process may take some time to complete.

Stop SQL cluster.
You can only start the cluster when its status is "available".

Cluster stopping process may take some time to complete.

get.description:
Get List of Extend App using SQL database by given studio/publisher namespace and the SQL cluster resourceId.
- <code>available</code> : The cluster is accessible. 
- <code>updating</code> : The cluster is being modified and is not yet accessible (e.g., updating min/max ACU). 
- <code>failed</code> : The cluster failed to provision or is in an error state and not accessible. 
- <code>stopping</code> : The cluster is in the process of stopping and will soon become inaccessible. 
- <code>stopped</code> : The cluster is stopped and not accessible. 
- <code>starting</code> : The cluster is transitioning from stopped to running, or is rebooting. 
- <code>creating</code> : The cluster or instance is being created and is not yet accessible. 
- <code>deleting</code> : The cluster is in the process of being deleted and is not accessible. 
- <code>maintenance</code> : The cluster is undergoing maintenance operations and is not accessible. 
- <code>unknown</code> : The cluster status is not recognized 
- <code>configuring-replica</code> : The cluster is configuring replica instances for multi-AZ deployment.
get.summary: Get List of Extend App using SQL

get.description:
Get List of Extend App using NoSQL database by given studio/publisher namespace and the NoSQL cluster resourceId.
- <code>starting</code> : The cluster is transitioning from stopped to running, or is rebooting. 
- <code>unknown</code> : The cluster status is not recognized 
- <code>available</code> : The cluster is accessible. 
- <code>maintenance</code> : The cluster is undergoing maintenance operations and is not accessible. 
- <code>updating</code> : The cluster is being modified and is not yet accessible (e.g., updating min/max DCU). 
- <code>creating</code> : The cluster or instance is being created and is not yet accessible. 
- <code>deleting</code> : The cluster is in the process of being deleted and is not accessible. 
- <code>failed</code> : The cluster failed to provision or is in an error state and not accessible. 
- <code>stopping</code> : The cluster is in the process of stopping and will soon become inaccessible. 
- <code>stopped</code> : The cluster is stopped and not accessible.
get.summary: Get List of Extend App using NoSQL

delete.description:
Remove a user from the notification subscription using either user ID or email address. 
Only one of them should be provided.
delete.summary: Remove other person subscription by user ID or email address

get.description:
Request param:
- notificationType : type of the subscribed app notification
	- values: 
	    - all
		- app-down
		- critical-vulnerability

Get a list of the app notification subscriber
get.summary: Get a list of the app notification subscriber

get.description:
Required permission : <code>ADMIN:NAMESPACE:{namespace}:EXTEND:APP [READ]</code>

Returns the list of debug-ready pods and exposed services for the given extend app.

A pod is included only when it is in <code>Running</code> phase and has an
<code>extend-proxy-sidecar</code> container.
get.summary: Get debug info for an extend app

put.description:
Required permission : <code>ADMIN:NAMESPACE:{namespace}:EXTEND:APP [UPDATE]</code>

Enable or disable the remote debug sidecar container for the specified extend app.

When enabled and the app is in <code>deployment-running</code> status, a new deployment will be
triggered automatically to apply the debug container configuration.

This operation is rejected with <code>409 Conflict</code> if the app is currently in a
transitional state (app-creating, app-removing, app-stopping).
put.summary: Enable or disable remote debug mode for an extend app

get.description: Returns step rows for the current or last tracked operation.
get.summary: Get in-progress long-running operation steps for the extend app (stepper)
